## Converters.tuple() function

Creates a [Converter](./ts-utils.converter.md) that converts an array to a strongly-typed tuple, using the supplied tuple of [Converters](./ts-utils.converter.md) to convert each element.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export declare function tuple<T extends readonly Converter<unknown, TC>[], TC = unknown>(converters: [...T]): Converter<ConverterResultTypes<T>, TC>;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter


</th><th>

Type


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

converters


</td><td>

\[...T\]


</td><td>

A tuple of [Converters](./ts-utils.converter.md) defining the expected types for each element of the tuple.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

[Converter](./ts-utils.converter.md)<!-- -->&lt;[ConverterResultTypes](./ts-utils.conversion.converterresulttypes.md)<!-- -->&lt;T&gt;, TC&gt;

A [Converter](./ts-utils.converter.md) that returns a strongly-typed tuple.

## Remarks

The resulting [Converter](./ts-utils.converter.md) returns [Success](./ts-utils.success.md) with a tuple containing the converted values if all conversions succeed. Returns [Failure](./ts-utils.failure.md) with an error message if the source is not an array, has the wrong number of elements, or any element conversion fails.

## Example


```typescript
const converter = tuple([Converters.string, Converters.number, Converters.boolean]);
// Type is Converter<[string, number, boolean]>

converter.convert(['hello', 42, true]);
// Returns Success with ['hello', 42, true]
```
